mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns. The mechanism operates continuously whenever rate trajectories are observable.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.15% as of t-1mo suggests low-rate environment
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns during periods of declining FEDFUNDS are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period, it signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as equity prices adjust to lower earnings expectations.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand. When both series decline simultaneously by more than 2% month-over-month, equity valuations compress due to lower expected corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline > 2% MoM simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 421399M and HOUST at 890K as of t-2mo
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 50% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) above its 12-month moving average signals robust supply-side capacity utilization and productivity growth. This sustains corporate profit margins and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days in expansion regimes.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with rising capacity utilization
failure_condition: Mean 60-day forward returns when INDPRO > MA are not significantly higher than when INDPRO < MA (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerating to below 100K new jobs per month signals labor market weakening that precedes broader economic slowdown. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as investors anticipate earnings pressure.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.025 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 2%, a real rate compression occurs that signals monetary policy lag. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as markets anticipate future tightening and higher discount rates.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 232.77 and FEDFUNDS at 0.15% as of t-1mo
regime_hint: monetary policy lag with rising inflation
failure_condition: The interaction term (inflation × low rates) is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in predicting 20-day returns, or SPX produces positive returns in > 50% of co-triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerating to below 2% annualized signals late-cycle economic slowdown. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as equity valuations compress in anticipation of earnings deceleration and potential recession.
failure_condition: Mean 60-day forward returns during sub-2% growth periods are not significantly lower than during above-2% growth (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.035 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) rising above 1.2 million annualized units signals strong residential investment and forward-looking demand strength. This produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as markets interpret housing strength as confirmation of economic resilience and corporate earnings sustainability.
regime_hint: expansion regime with strong housing demand
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 18-month rolling window.
